detail: SAFETY MODEL (the owner-controlled belt). Two layers: (1) bubblewrap sandbox — whole FS read-only, only the job workspace + /tmp writable, and .env/secrets/keys.vault/~/.ssh/every /opt/*/.env MASKED unreadable; verified a write outside the workspace fails ('Read-only file system') and cat .env returns nothing. (2) intent classifier: SAFE runs; RISKY (push/deploy/publish/external-POST/rsync/scp) is refused UNLESS this job was owner-/approved, then it runs; PROTECTED (systemctl/docker/apt/pip -g/firewall/git remote+config/secret paths/reboot) is HARD-blocked ALWAYS, even for an approved job — infra surgery stays keyboard/architect-only. Outward flow: agent builds the reversible part freely, calls request_owner_approval(action) which parks a follow-up job; owner /approve runs it with approved=True on the SAME workspace (executor _workspace_for parses 'workspace:' from the [approved-action] task). Kill switch: touch workspace/KILL or WORKSPACE_AGENT_DISABLED=1. Command cap 80/job, 300s timeout, output clipped. OP NOTE: heavy multi-step builds can exhaust the free Gemini tier mid-loop; execute() now still delivers whatever files were built + the zip instead of only the quota error. A paid Gemini key (via the vault) or the Claude council path makes long builds robust.

Fix: /opt/research-lab/brain_bridge.py mirrors every accepted finding into (a) decisions.jsonl via shared_memory.remember(scope=shared) so it is recall-able, and (b) a self-pruning Radar open-findings section (RADAR:START/END markers) in SHARED_CONTEXT.md, the mandatory-read doc. TELEGRAM VOICE+BRAIN overhaul (2026-07-12, commit 9bbb305) + a CRITICAL latent bug found: the GEMINI/GOOGLE API keys in .env are DEAD (every direct Gemini call 400s "API key not valid"). Impact: the system silently limped on Groq for chat, but the tools/hands, research(google-search-grounded) and browser executor lanes call genai.Client(GEMINI_API_KEY) DIRECTLY -> those lanes are BROKEN until a valid key is set. Fixes shipped: (1) brain -> smart cascade with a Groq llama-3.3-70b floor so conversation reliably uses the strongest WORKING free model (auto-upgrades to Gemini/Claude when a valid key returns); (2) STT -> ElevenLabs Scribe first (best AZ ~3.1% WER, works on free tier) -> Groq whisper-v3 -> Gemini; (3) voice-reply (voice-in->AZ-voice-out) fully built but GATED behind VOICE_REPLIES=1 because ALL free TTS is blocked (ElevenLabs free tier forbids API TTS; OpenAI quota exhausted; Gemini keys dead). THE ONE HIGH-LEVERAGE FIX: a fresh free Google AI Studio key (aistudio.google.com) via /setkey GEMINI_API_KEY <v> -> un-breaks tools/research/browser, upgrades brain to Gemini 2.5 Pro, AND enables free Gemini TTS voice replies (then set VOICE_REPLIES=1). The real-Claude brain (MIC_BRAIN=claude) needs claude CLI login on the VPS (currently: Not logged in).

AD-PLATFORM MCP CAPABILITY shipped (2026-07-12): the engine can now SPEAK MCP, not just describe it. gateway/mcp_client.py = generic Streamable-HTTP JSON-RPC MCP client (stdlib only, initialize/tools-list/tools-call, SSE + session-id + bearer, 401 raises McpAuthError carrying the WWW-Authenticate scopes so the human is told exactly what is missing). gateway/ad_mcp.py = connectors for all four MCP servers OUR OWN LAB found: Meta Ads (mcp.facebook.com/ads, official, 2026-04-29), TikTok Ads, AdRoll, Canva. MONEY GUARD: tools matching create/update/delete/pause/resume/budget/bid/launch/publish are classified WRITE and refused unless approved=True, which only the owner-authed human-checkpoint path may pass - an LLM must never set it. WHY A TOKEN, NOT A BROWSER LOGIN: Meta OAuth metadata advertises only authorization_code+refresh_token (NO client_credentials), so no machine-to-machine mint is possible; Metas own answer for always-on server use is a System User token (Business Manager -> System users -> Generate token, scopes ads_management/ads_read/business_management), sent as a plain bearer. Selenium was considered and REJECTED for this: it would require holding the owners Facebook master password and logging in from a datacenter IP (checkpoint risk + account lockout) - strictly worse than a scoped, revocable token. LAST STEP (owner-only): /setkey META_ADS_TOKEN <token>, then ad_mcp status flips needs-token -> live and Telegram/panel/executor can drive it. Live-verified: transport reaches the real Meta server and parses its 401+scopes; write guard blocks pause_ad before any network call. TWO REPO FINDINGS: (1) pytest was MISSING on the VPS entirely (installed into .venv) so the run-the-suite-before-commit rule was unenforceable server-side; (2) a bare pytest at repo root is POISONED - meta-capi/gateway.py shadows the gateway/ package on sys.path (81 failed/21 collection errors); the correct invocation is pytest tests/ = 308 passed, 2 known pre-existing test_flora_ai env fails.

HANDOFF -> fleet: gateway/tools/extract.py shipped hard-depending on lxml/bs4/pypdf which were NOT in any requirements file — on a host without lxml, BeautifulSoup(html,'lxml') raised FeatureNotFound, fetch()'s escalation try/except swallowed it, and EVERY page silently took the slow browser path instead of requests (5 tests failed on corporate). Fixed: _soup() helper degrades to stdlib html.parser; deps declared in requirements-panel.txt (bs4, lxml, pypdf, pytest). Lesson: a new module's deps must land in requirements in the SAME commit, and a swallowed parser error must never silently change routing. Also doit: added doctor()/--check — it now reports 'no provider session to inherit' as one clean sentence + the single human step, instead of launching a browser that must fail (root cause of the RAPIDAPI_KEY dead end: the operator has ZERO rapidapi.com cookies in any profile = never signed in; Google session DOES exist in Chrome Profile 3, so a one-click 'Sign in with Google' in the NORMAL browser unlocks full autonomy afterwards).

summary: META ADS — THE SYSTEM CAN NOW ACT (2026-07-13). CORRECTION TO MY OWN EARLIER ADVICE: the system ALREADY held a full-scope Meta token. .env META_ACCESS_TOKEN + META_GRAPH_ACCESS_TOKEN both carry ads_management + ads_read + business_management (verified via /me/permissions), and META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID=act_1022941045861923 reads live (Xalq Sigorta ad account 1, USD, amount_spent 2301243). So the earlier instruction to mint a System User token was WRONG and unnecessary - ALWAYS check .env + existing connectors before telling the owner to go fetch credentials. HARD FACT: the official Meta MCP (mcp.facebook.com/ads) is CLIENT-ONLY - its dynamic client registration returns Dynamic registration is not available for this client and its OAuth advertises no client_credentials grant, so ONLY allowlisted clients (Claude/ChatGPT) can hold a token. Our server can never drive it; do not try again. Server-side Meta goes through the GRAPH API. BUILT: ads-studio/connectors/meta_write.py - the missing POST verb on top of the mature read-only connectors/meta.py (reuses its retry/backoff, error classification, token redaction; does not fork it). Two-step by design: propose() reads the CURRENT state and returns a human-readable plan; execute() raises WriteBlocked unless approved=True, which may ONLY come from the owner-authed checkpoint (Telegram /approve N, panel) - an LLM must never set it. Only REVERSIBLE ops exist: pause, resume, set_daily_budget. NO create, NO delete. Budget bounded to [100, 100000] minor units so a hallucinated number cannot become a five-figure daily spend. LIVE-VERIFIED on the real account: campaigns list, propose pause on the ACTIVE Awareness campaign, execute blocked unapproved, budget guard refused 999999. 14 guard tests (an unapproved write must never reach the network); pytest tests/ = 322 passed, 2 known pre-existing test_flora_ai env fails. NEXT: wire propose/execute into the Telegram /approve queue + panel so it is omnichannel.

PROVIDER/BRAIN REALITY (2026-07-13 audit): the reason the Telegram brain is not premium is that EVERY paid API key in .env is dead/empty: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=10-char placeholder (401), OPENAI_API_KEY valid but OUT OF QUOTA (429), GEMINI/GOOGLE keys invalid (400). Only Groq generates. BUT the CLIs are subscription-authed and WORK: codex is logged in via ChatGPT (codex exec returns real answers, ~9s, agentic — good for WORK lanes not chat); claude CLI NOT logged in; gemini CLI ineligible (UNSUPPORTED_CLIENT free tier). Fixes: claude_bridge.is_available() now requires real login (no wasted calls); MIC_BRAIN=claude set (falls back to Groq until login, auto-becomes Claude the moment creds appear); Desktop launcher "🔑 Beyin Girişi (Claude)" runs claude setup-token over SSH for one-click login. NEXT high-value: (1) route the tools/research/browser WORK lanes through Codex (they currently call the DEAD Gemini key directly -> broken); (2) a fresh free Google AI Studio key un-breaks Gemini everywhere + enables free Gemini TTS. OpenClaw/Hermes lesson: our supervisor+one-mic+Markdown-memory already matches OpenClaw gateway; adoptable from Hermes(NousResearch) = turn successful jobs into reusable Markdown SKILL files (we have reflect_job lessons, not skill extraction yet).

ADS LANE — ALIM TALKS, THE SYSTEM ACTS (2026-07-13). Owner directive: I do not want to speak CLI (no /approve 3, no campaign ids) - I want to talk to the system in Telegram exactly like I talk to Claude. Built gateway/ads_agent.py: a plain-Azerbaijani lane over the Meta ad account. (1) cheap keyword gate wants_ads() so normal chat is never hijacked (an ad-account noun + an operational cue, and content asks like reklam metni yaz are excluded and still go to the content lane); (2) the FREE llm_router extracts intent -> {action,campaign,amount}; if it decides the sentence is not really about the ad account it returns none and the message falls through to the normal conversational brain; (3) READS answer instantly (campaign list, spend); (4) WRITES resolve the campaign BY NAME (never an id) via exact->substring->fuzzy match, ask -Hansi kampaniyani nezerde tutursan?- when ambiguous instead of guessing, build a plan with meta_write.propose() (which reads CURRENT live state), save it to data/ads_plans/job-N.json, and return needs_approval -> the worker parks the job. NOTHING is executed. (5) On the approved re-run the SAVED plan is re-verified against live state - if the campaign drifted since the owner saw it (status changed), it REFUSES and asks again, so the owner always approves exactly what he was shown. Wired into executor.py as a rail ahead of the generic checkpoint (it can show WHAT changes, not just -risky task-). bot.py now accepts PLAIN-LANGUAGE approval: a bare he/beli/tesdiq/ok approves and yox/legv/imtina rejects - owner-only, whole-message exact match, and ONLY when exactly one job is parked (zero -> it is just conversation and falls through; more than one -> it asks which). /approve N still works. LIVE-VERIFIED end-to-end on the real account: -kampaniyalari gester- returned the real campaign list; -Awareness kampaniyasini dayandir- parked with a human preview (Kampaniya: Awareness / Indiki veziyyet: ACTIVE / Edilecek: DAYANDIRILACAQ / Tesdiq ucun: he) and executed NOTHING. 15 guard tests. PRE-EXISTING FAILURE FOUND (not mine, proven by stashing): tests/test_front_office.py fails on the VPS because runtime dirs published/ and review-queue/ (the latter created by the research labs content_studio) are not on the services.json shelf - the sonarzum rule is correctly flagging two real unregistered organs.

BRAIN + LEARNING + CROSS-TWIN (2026-07-13, commits 349b25c/58356a6/7200453): (1) Two-account Claude auto-failover — bridge rotates across the operators two Claude subscriptions, rests a capped account ~5h and fails over, sticks with whichever works; tokens in git-ignored data/private_context/claude_accounts.json; add via scripts/add_claude_account.sh (.ps1 for Windows) or the Mac launcher (loops for 2 accounts). setup-token PRINTS a token (no creds file) — that is why earlier login did not stick. (2) Launchers now travel to BOTH twins: scripts/install_launchers.py (platform-aware: Windows=localhost, Mac=SSH tunnel) hooked into START_MARKETING_OS.ps1 boot -> work PC Desktop gets the buttons on open. Standing rule saved. (3) Hermes learning loop: gateway/skills.py learns a reusable skill card from each successful WORK job and injects matching cards into the tools/research prompts next time; local per-machine (data/skills/, git-ignored) to protect sync. NEXT: route the tools/research/browser lanes through Codex (ChatGPT-authed, works) since the Gemini key they call is dead.

SUITE FULLY GREEN + FLORA TRANSFERRED (2026-07-13). Two real bugs fixed, both cross-machine. (1) FRONT-DOOR GAP: organ_coverage() flagged published/ and review-queue/ as unaccounted organs. They are OUTPUT SINKS, not organs - published/ is where publish_site results land (its front door IS registered, as the published-sites static server on 8767, but under external, and organ_coverage only reads services/capabilities); review-queue/ is where the research labs content_studio drops drafts for human review. Both got a conscious audit_ignore_dirs entry in services.json, exactly as the sonarzum rule prescribes. (2) FLORA WAS BROKEN EVERYWHERE EXCEPT THE WINDOWS PC - and it is our PAID subscription. claude-agents/.claude/settings.json is git-tracked (it TRAVELS) and hardcodes the work PCs own absolute path: C:\Users\a.alirzayev\...\npx.cmd. This violates the engine/data boundary (the ENGINE must be machine-agnostic). Worse, a POSIX Path does not split on backslash, so Path(win_path).name returned the WHOLE string on Linux/macOS - which defeated every name check: doctor reported the integration broken and _flora_transport misread stdio_proxy_to_http as plain stdio. Fixed in gateway/flora_ai.py: _basename() splits either OS, and _resolve_command() resolves the way a launcher would (configured path -> repo-vendored npx -> npx on PATH) and reports settings_command_resolved, so the doctor states what would ACTUALLY run. Their own tests asserted settings_command_available is True, so this completes an intent that was already written (_portable_npx_candidates existed but was never wired into the availability decision). 3 regression guards added. (3) TRANSFERRED flora to the Mac: ~/.claude.json mcpServers.flora = {command: <absolute nvm npx>, args: [-y, mcp-remote, https://agents.flora.ai/mcp]} - same paid subscription. An absolute path is CORRECT there because ~/.claude.json is machine-local and does NOT travel, and it is NECESSARY because node lives under nvm, whose PATH GUI apps never see. Owner does one browser OAuth to activate. NOTE the work PC also has elevenlabs, filesystem, n8n-mcp, qdrant, browser-use, memory, context7 MCPs that were never transferred to the Mac. RESULT: pytest tests/ = 404 passed, 0 failed - the suite is fully green for the first time (the 2 -known pre-existing flora env fails- in earlier decisions were this bug all along, not an environment quirk).

BRAIN FULLY CONNECTED (2026-07-14, commits e376e52/5642cca). ROOT CAUSE of the weak brain found + fixed: the dead 10-char ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env (loaded by load_env) SHADOWED the valid CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN -> every claude -p turn 401d and fell to Groq. Fix: claude_bridge strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN from the child env when a rotation token is set. Now the Telegram/panel conversational brain is REAL Claude (chat:claude-code) with 3-account auto-failover. GEMINI verdict: cannot connect on subscription — Google DEPRECATED the free Gemini Code Assist CLI for individuals (IneligibleTierError -> migrate to Antigravity antigravity.google); only a fresh AI Studio API key works (the .env one is dead). CODEX: authed to ChatGPT and works (model gpt-5.5) but usage quota is spent until ~Jul 26. WORK/BUILD lane (was calling dead Gemini) now falls over: Gemini(dead)->Codex(when quota back)->claude_bridge.build (agentic claude -p acceptEdits in the job workspace, rotation) which works TODAY (verified: built a real file). Test suites MUST run process-isolated (python run_tests.py). Root pytest sees 8 rival config.py + meta-capi/gateway.py shadowing the gateway/ package + seo/http.py shadowing stdlib http; one interpreter makes them overwrite each other in sys.modules, so green tests report red (classic: 'module gateway has no attribute _client_ip'). Never refactor the sub-tools to force a flat pytest run - isolation is the design. pytest.ini scopes bare pytest to the collision-free root suite. Baseline: 6 suites / 504 tests green.

ENCODING TRAP (2026-07-14, commit be077d0): subprocess.run(text=True) with no encoding= decodes with the LOCALE default = cp1252 on the Windows twin, which has no letter 'i' (U+0131, dotless i). Azerbaijani is the house language, so every 'claude -p' turn crashed with UnicodeEncodeError -> claude_bridge marked 'unavailable' -> the whole system silently fell back to free Groq for weeks. The premium brain was never down, it was never reachable. BUG CLASS: AST sweep found 13 sites (claude_bridge, executor/codex, sense/git, engine_sync, sync_engine, postpull, panel, bot, council, workspace_agent) - all pinned to encoding='utf-8', errors='replace'. Guard: tests/test_subprocess_encoding.py walks the AST, fails on reintroduction. CRITICAL FOR THE LINUX TWIN: this bug is INVISIBLE on UTF-8 locales - it can only be caught structurally, never by running it there. RULE: an 'unavailable' capability is a claim, not a diagnosis - always ask WHY.

RAPIDAPI AUTH IS AUTH0, NOT A rapidapi.com COOKIE (2026-07-14, commit 22aa82a). doit reported 'no rapidapi session' while the operator was logged in via Chrome, because RapidAPI authenticates through Auth0: a logged-in profile has auth0.com cookies + Google SSO, ZERO rapidapi.com cookies. Fix: recipe session_markers=('rapidapi.com','auth0'), find_profile tries markers in order -> now finds Profile 3. LESSON: never gate browser-session detection on the first-party domain alone; SaaS auth often lives in a third-party IdP (Auth0/Okta) cookie + localStorage. The key fetch stays on the governed rail (GATEWAY_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS + owner Telegram); the assistant does not scan a live credential store unattended.

PREMIUM BRAIN HAS TWO SWITCHES, NOT ONE (2026-07-14). 'Everything falls to Groq' had TWO independent causes, both needed fixing: (1) claude_bridge crashed on Azerbaijani 'i' (U+0131) because subprocess.run(text=True) used cp1252 on Windows -> bridge marked unavailable (commit be077d0, 13 sites). (2) MIC_BRAIN defaults to 'free' -> the conversational Telegram/mic path (executor._converse) never even CALLS claude_bridge; it goes straight to the free router (Gemini dead -> Groq). Fixing the encoding bug alone does NOTHING for everyday chat quality, because that path doesn't touch the bridge unless MIC_BRAIN=claude. To actually get the premium brain answering Telegram: set MIC_BRAIN=claude in .env AND fix the encoding. Verified: executor._converse now returns 'chat:claude-code'. NOTE: with the failover store empty, MIC_BRAIN=claude burns the single account's 5h cap then falls to Groq via the built-in fallback -> register the 2nd account (claude setup-token) for true 24/7. LESSON: a capability can be broken in layers; when it 'still doesn't work' after one fix, trace the ACTUAL call path the user hits, don't assume.

AI RADAR (4th radar) CANCELLED AS-IS (2026-07-14). A prior session built gateway/radar.py: a broad HF+GitHub+GitLab+RSS scan -> free-LLM digest -> weekly owner Telegram brief. Pro audit verdict = net-negative as-is, reverted (code parked, recoverable). WHY: (1) FRAGMENTATION - it was a 4th radar overlapping signal_radar (scan->digest->lab loop) and hf_radar (HF sourcing) while BYPASSING their governance (official-source gate, security scoring, prototype backlog); violates the charter 'reinforce existing modules, do not create disconnected side tools'. (2) POLLUTED capabilities.md - the curated 'router brain' read before EVERY task - by appending ~40 lines/week of weak digest (active harm to a hot context doc). (3) WEAK OUTPUT - tier='cheap' -> Groq (Gemini dead) produced circular generic text that violated its own good prompt's anti-generic rules. (4) knowledge.py added +50 tokens/task, untested. (5) no test. THE IDEA IS GOOD (owner-facing weekly cross-vendor landscape brief is a real gap the 3 governed radars don't fill) - rebuild it PROPERLY as a reinforcement that CONSUMES the existing radars, digests on the premium brain, writes only to data/radar/ (never capabilities.md), with a test. RULE: an autonomously-built feature must still pass the 'reinforce not fragment' + 'no hot-doc pollution' bar before adoption.

CLAUDE IS THE BRAIN EVERYWHERE (2026-07-14, commits d539755 + 5a08bcc). Owner directive: 'yaln; claude olsun her yerde beyin ve emr veren merkez'. Implementation: llm_router.complete() smart tier now tries the Claude SUBSCRIPTION first (claude_bridge.complete = stateless one-shot, account rotation, newest model by default = Fable, CLAUDE_BRIDGE_MODEL to pin), free API cascade (Groq) is the resilience floor for when every account is capped. Gated by BRAIN_CLAUDE_FIRST (default on). POLICY: cheap tier (mechanical bulk: scrape/parse/score) STAYS FREE by design so it never cannibalizes the 5h cap that the thinking needs; CLAUDE_EVERYWHERE=1 overrides to route even cheap through Claude. JSON stays free (CLI has no structured-output guarantee). Conversational path was already claude via MIC_BRAIN=claude. Radar digest bumped cheap->smart so its brief is premium. Tests pin BRAIN_CLAUDE_FIRST=0 so the suite never shells out to real claude -p. RULE: 'the brain' = thinking/synthesis/decisions/conversation/command = Claude; mechanical grunt = free floor. This is the sustainable way to maximize Claude-brain-time on one account.

GEMINI RESTORED + FULL VOICE LOOP LIVE (2026-07-14, commit 90c0a65). Alim supplied a fresh AI Studio key (new AQ.* format, 53 chars) after the first one was auto-disabled by Google as leaked (403 - never paste keys in chat). FABLE IS DEAD ON THE SUBSCRIPTION -> IT WAS SILENTLY BREAKING THE SELF-IMPROVEMENT LOOPS (2026-07-17). Probed all 3 Claude accounts against claude-fable-5: every one returns api 429 'Usage credits are required for this model.' So Fable is NOT a daily/weekly cap that resets - it now needs PAID pay-as-you-go credits, not covered by the subscription plan. This is the concrete answer to Alim's 'why don't I see the self-improving steps': the three lab loops were pinned to Fable and had been FAILING for weeks - builder-cron log 2026-07-07: 'You are out of usage credits. /model to switch models'; engineer-cron 2026-07-09: 429. The MAIN lab.py was already on sonnet/haiku (it learned fine yesterday, 5 findings incl. Sora/Muse Video), but builder.py/content_studio.py/engineer.py inline defaults + their config JSONs + .env CLAUDE_COUNCIL_MODEL were all still claude-fable-5. FIX: retargeted every live Fable MODEL pin -> claude-sonnet-4-6 (the proven working subscription model; .env backed up to .env.bak.fable-fix.*, engineer-seen.json left alone - those 'grooverLab/fable' strings are GitHub repo names, not model ids). Smoke-tested: engineer research call returns ENGINEER_OK on sonnet, no 429. Supervisor restarted for the new council model. NOTE: the chat brain was NEVER affected - claude_bridge runs 'claude -p' with NO --model flag, so it uses the account default (sonnet/opus), not Fable. LESSON: a model pin is a silent single point of failure - when a provider changes a model's billing tier, every hard-coded pin to it dies at once and only the logs show it; audit model pins the same way we audit dead keys (shadowing). To re-enable Fable, add usage credits to an account and flip the pins back - keep sonnet as the safe default.

MODEL LADDER, NOT STATIC PINS (2026-07-17, Alim's design correction). Alim rejected hard-pinning a model: 'model availability is dynamic; default to the LATEST + STRONGEST and, like this chat, auto-step-DOWN one rung the moment a model runs out.' Implemented in lab.claude_call (the ONE helper every loop shares: lab/engineer/builder/content_studio/champion): a MODEL_LADDER (env CLAUDE_MODEL_LADDER, default opus-4-8 -> sonnet-5 -> sonnet-4-6 -> haiku-4-5) is walked ceiling-first; on a stepdown cue (429 / 'usage credits are required' / usage|weekly|session limit / not available) it drops to the next rung instead of failing; transient errors retry the same rung. The passed `model` is the CEILING (a cheap caller passes a low rung and stays cheap; a DEAD or unknown pin like the old fable auto-starts at the strongest). Probed live first (one account, plan tier is representative): opus-4-8, sonnet-5, sonnet-4-6, haiku-4-5 are ALL plan-available; only fable is CREDIT-GATED. Bumped the THINKING roles to the strongest (opus-4-8): lab research_model, engineer/builder/content_studio inline + config JSONs, and .env CLAUDE_COUNCIL_MODEL; judge roles stay cheap (haiku/sonnet) and cascade too. PROVEN end-to-end: with CLAUDE_MODEL_LADDER='claude-fable-5,claude-opus-4-8' a call asking for the dead fable returned CASCADE_OK — it stepped fable(credit-gated) -> opus automatically. Supervisor restarted for the new council model. TRADE-OFF: opus-first spends the shared subscription cap faster than sonnet; the cascade degrades gracefully when it caps, and CLAUDE_MODEL_LADDER lets us lower the ceiling to sonnet-5 if chat quality ever suffers under load. This supersedes yesterday's 'retarget fable -> sonnet-4-6' static fix: the pin approach just moved the single point of failure; the ladder removes it. LESSON: for a resource whose availability changes under you (model tiers, quotas), build a cascade with a tunable ceiling, never a fixed pin.

MULTI-STEP PLANNER shipped (2026-07-17, commit b648c9d, gateway/executor.py). The one real agentic gap: the live path picked ONE lane per task, so a chained marketing ask ('research the latest X, THEN draft posts about it') could not run in a single turn. _plan_and_run decomposes such a task into <=4 ordered steps, each mapped to an EXISTING lane (research=Gemini google_search grounding / content=_content_generate / reason=_converse), runs them in sequence THREADING each result into the next (bounded to 6k chars), then synthesises one Azerbaijani deliverable. Deliberately NOT CrewAI/LangChain (would fragment; charter = reinforce existing modules) and NOT the orphaned orchestrator/graph.py PoC (redundant with the checkpoint executor already grew). One failing step does not sink the chain. Also extracted the inline research block into _research_grounded so execute() and the planner share one source of truth. VERIFIED: 5 unit tests (order, context-threading, single-step fallback, one-bad-step resilience) + full tests/ suite green (489 passed) + a LIVE end-to-end run that degraded gracefully when the research lane hit Gemini's spend cap (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED - separate issue, Alim's ai.studio/spend) and STILL delivered two brand-voiced content ideas with post text + an image prompt, honestly flagging that live trends were unavailable rather than inventing them. AZ VOCAL PRONUNCIATION — THE SECRET IS IPA, NOT A SECRET MODEL (2026-07-18, music-lab discovery). Ear-test verdict: Lyria sings AZ decently but drops the back vowel ı (/ɯ/) to front i (sabahın -> sabahin). How the pros do native AZ: (1) ElevenLabs v3 officially supports Azerbaijani (aze, 70+ langs) AND accepts IPA inline (/sabahˈɯn/, 80-90pct consistency) + a pronunciation-dictionary feature -> you hand it phonemes, not raw text. (2) Suno: AZ NOT on its ~50-lang list, sings it Turkish-ish, mixes languages. (3) Lyria: no IPA input surface, so we STEER via orthographic respelling. OUR REPLICABLE METHOD: espeak-ng natively knows AZ (brew/apt install espeak-ng; espeak-ng -v az --ipa -q gives the target: sabahın->sabahˈɯn, gecə->ɟˈedʒæ, müştəri->myʃtæɾˈi). Built music-lab/az_pronounce/g2p.py: word -> AZ IPA -> flags trouble phonemes (ɯ/ı, æ/ə, y/ü, ø/ö, ɟ/g, c/k) -> auto-proposes respelling candidates (ı->u/y etc). Replaces per-word manual guessing. Bake-off confirmed live: for sabahın, Alim ear-picked the u-nudge (sabahun ~ /sabahˈun/, closest back vowel to /ɯ/). NEXT: espeak-ng NOT yet on VPS (needed there for the systematic path); for ElevenLabs lane feed IPA directly; build an AZ pronunciation dictionary as the moat asset. Also caught: günaydın is TURKISH; AZ morning greeting is sabahın xeyir (TR->AZ bleed guard added to pron_map).

CREW vs COUNCIL — CONCEPTUAL CORRECTION (2026-07-18, Alim caught the mis-mapping; supersedes the crew=council line in the two prior 2026-07-18 audit entries). A CrewAI CREW is NOT multi-vendor LLM voting. Per current docs (v1.15.x, collaboration.mdx / custom-manager-agent.mdx): a crew = specialized ROLE-BASED agents, each with role+goal+backstory+its own tools+optionally its own llm, orchestrated either SEQUENTIALLY (tasks in order, output threaded, agents get DelegateWorkTool/AskQuestionTool to ask each other) or HIERARCHICALLY (a MANAGER agent, manager_llm/manager_agent, decomposes -> delegates to specialists -> validates). Often ALL agents share ONE llm — the power is specialization+tool-ownership+manager delegation, NOT vendor diversity. Therefore our gateway/council.py (Codex+Claude+Gemini each critiquing the SAME question, chair synthesises) is an ENSEMBLE/DEBATE pattern, a DIFFERENT and complementary technique CrewAI does not emphasise — it is NOT our crew analog and should not be positioned as our multi-agent layer. THE REAL CREW ANALOG in our system = the _plan_and_run planner (thin: 3 generic lanes research/content/reason, no persona, no per-agent tools, no manager delegation/validation) PLUS the 15 STUDIOS, which ARE genuine domain specialists (ads/seo/cx/price/atelier/influencer/media_studio...) with own data+tools+endpoints — arguably STRONGER than in-process CrewAI agents, BUT nothing orchestrates them as a manager-led team (decompose a goal -> delegate to the right specialist studio -> thread -> manager validates -> deliver). THAT hierarchical studio-supervisor is the actual GAP, and it is the layer where importing CrewAI could genuinely earn its weight (free manager+DelegateWorkTool+AskQuestionTool+structured task-context+guardrails, wrapping studios as agents/tools) — my blanket no-CrewAI verdict rejected the RIGHT thing (fragmenting the chat executor) but for the WRONG layer (I never evaluated CrewAI for studio-orchestration). OPENCODE reality: NOT installed on the VPS (opencode: command not found); the council _ask_opencode hardcodes a WINDOWS node path (video-studio/tools/node-v24.15.0-win-x64) + env[Path] — it was written for the Windows work-PC twin only, positioned as the free EXECUTOR not a 4th opinion, and joins the debate panel ONLY with a non-Gemini free model (diversity guard). So on the VPS brain OpenCode currently adds nothing; it is a Windows-twin asset. MODERN AGENTIC-OS PRACTICE (2025-26) converges on: supervisor/manager + specialist sub-agents-with-own-tools (crew/hierarchical) as the BACKBONE, with multi-model DEBATE reserved for high-stakes judgment/verification — exactly the emphasis we got backwards (we built debate as the backbone, left the crew as a thin planner).

ROUTING RESEARCH — HOW THE MASTERS DO IT (2026-07-19, web + lab consensus). Bare keyword routing at the front door is the NAMED anti-pattern in every 2026 production guide — exactly our 4 misroutes in 3 days. Production standard is a LAYERED cascade: (1) deterministic rules ONLY for explicit/obvious cases (slash-commands); (2) a cheap classifier — small LLM or embedding head — WITH CONVERSATION CONTEXT for everything else; (3) low confidence -> fail OPEN to the main conversational agent (never to a specialist). Hierarchical triage (a supervisor/router-of-routers) beats one flat router as destinations grow; handoffs pass structured context so the specialist never loses the thread (our crew synthesis grounding = this). The strongest systems (Claude Code itself, OpenAI triage agents) go further: THE MODEL IS THE ROUTER — the front agent decides handoffs as tool calls, no pre-classification at all. Anthropic guidance: router as lightweight as the task allows; a good router cuts LLM cost 30-70%. CONCRETE NEXT STEP for us: replace the 4 keyword lists (_TOOL_HINTS/_FANOUT_CUES/_PLAN_CUES/_CREW_HEAVY_CUES) with ONE haiku-tier classify call {chat|system_ops|marketing_deliverable|build|research} fed the last 2 turns; slash-commands stay deterministic; low-confidence -> chat-claude. Sources: anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents, patronus.ai ai-agent-routing, botpress.com ai-agent-routing.
